person.name: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry

nameForms.fullText: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry

km.Berrynarbor, Devon, England: ប៊ើរយណារបូរ, ដិវ៉ុន, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry於1220年出生在英國戴文貝利納波爾。他至少有1個女兒。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry于1220年出生在英国戴文貝利納波爾。他至少有1个女儿。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Narbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, Dutch, and French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Nordberht, composed of the elements nord ‘north’ and berht ‘bright’.

variants.name: Nobert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Gaston, Henri, Huguette, Marcel.
French (Alsace and Lorraine) and German: variant of   Norbert  . This surname is very rare in both France and Germany.

variants.name: Norberto
meanings: Portuguese and Spanish: from the personal name Norberto, of ancient Germanic origin (see Norbert ).

variants.name: Nord
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Helmer, Alvar, Knute, Nels, Per.

Swedish and Danish: ornamental (Swedish) or topographic (Danish) name from nord ‘north’. As a topographic name it would have denoted someone who lived in the northern part of a village or to the north of a main settlement or someone who had migrated from the north.

Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Nor in Fjordane in western Norway (from nór ‘narrow waters, strait’), or from the farm name Nord ‘north’, denoting a farm lying to the north of another farm.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Wevers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from the city of Nevers in France.
Possibly an altered form of English Nevard or perhaps of its extinct variant Neverd. The surname Nevers is also found in Jamaica.

History: The majority of the American bearers of this surname trace their origin to Richard Nevers, born in 1666 in Woburn, MA. His origin is not known, but his surname is also documented in the forms Nevard and Neverds, which make it possible that Richard Nevers was of English origin.

variants.name: Nevard
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Nevard, from ancient Germanic Niwhard, from niw- ‘new’ + hard- ‘strong, hardy’. Compare Nevers .

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Berry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Béara ‘descendant of Béara’, a personal name of unexplained etymology; or, in some cases, perhaps an Anglicized form of Irish and Manx Ó Beargha. Compare Barry 1.
Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Barrie  .
English: habitational name from any of several places called in Devon named with Old English byrig, dative case of burh ‘fortified manor house, stronghold’, such as Berry Pomeroy and Berrynarbor.

variants.name: Berrey
meanings: English: variant of   Berry  .

variants.name: Barry
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Aidan, Ciaran, Eoin, John Patrick, Kaitlin, Kieran, Murphy, Aileen, Brigid, Caitlin.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Beargha ‘descendant of Beargh’, a byname meaning ‘plunderer’.

Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Báire ‘descendant of Báire’, a short form of either of two Gaelic personal names, Bairrfhionn or Fionnbharr, both meaning ‘fair-topped’. This may also be the source of the Manx name Barry, whose family's main seat was Ballavarry in Kirk Andreas.

variants.name: Bery
meanings: English: variant of   Berry  .
Hungarian (Béry): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Bér, in Baranya, Komárom, Nógrád, Pest, Vas, or Zala counties.

variants.name: Beery
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Ó Béara. See Berry and O'Berry .
Americanized form of Swiss German   Bieri  .

variants.name: Berrie
meanings: Scottish:
(Glasgow): from Middle English or Older Scots (atte) beri ‘(at the) manor house’, see Berry .
variant   Barrie  .

nameMeanings.name: Willmus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German and Dutch: variant of   Wilms  .

variants.name: Wilms
meanings: Contracted form of   Wilhelmina  , which has retained rather more currency (especially in America) than the full form of the name.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry was born in 1220, in Berrynarbor, Devon, England. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry est né en 1220, à Berrynarbor, Devon, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry wurde 1220 in Berrynarbor, Devon, England geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry è nato il nel 1220 nel Berrynarbor, Devon, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Berrynarbor, Devon, England: イギリスデボンバーリナルボル
ja.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berryは1220年にイギリスデボンバーリナルボルで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry(은)는 1220년 영국 데폰 베리나르볼에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry nasceu em 1220, em Berrynarbor, Devon, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry родился в 1220 г. в местности бирринарбор, девон, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Willmus Narbert de Wevers De Berry nació en 1220, en Berrynarbor, Devon, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en su pueblo.
